original: Roman Emperor Theodosius declares Christianity the state religion
Pope John XXII names Jan van Diest as Bishop of Utrecht
Uprising against Piero de' Medici in Florence
Stockholm Bloodbath begins as Danish forces successfully invade Sweden and execute approximately 100 people
French Roman Catholics and Huguenots sign treaty
Eighty Years' War: Pacification of Ghent - 17 Dutch provinces sign anti-Spanish covenant
Spanish troops under Bernardino de Mendoza conquer Doetinchem
Bodleian Library at Oxford University, founded by Thomas Bodley, formally opens
Battle of White Mountain, Prague: First major victory of the Catholic Habsburgs over the Protestant Alliance in the Thirty Years' War
Battle of the Sound: Swedish fleet prevails over the Dutch fleet during the 2nd Northern War
Vincent La Chapelle, master cook to various nobility and royalty, forms a Freemasons Lodge in the Netherlands
Bourbon whiskey is first distilled from corn by Elijah Craig in Bourbon County, Virginia (modern-day Kentucky)
Mount Holyoke Seminary in Massachusetts founded - 1st US college founded for women
King of Belgium Leopold I proclaims child labor laws (for 1889)
Battle of Mount Ivy, Kentucky: Union Commander General William "Bull" Nelson breaks up Confederate recruiting camp
English freighter Nisero stranded at Atjeh (crew taken hostage)
Montana admitted as 41st state of the Union
Bloody clashes take place in Athens following the translation of the Gospels into demotic Greek
Inventor and manufacturer Harvey Hubbell receives the first U.S. patent for a separable electric attachment plug
In a landmark decision for Australia, the Court of Conciliation and Arbitration rules that the Sunshine Harvester Work must pay 'fair and reasonable wages'
1st Washington State election in which women could vote
For the first time since 1894, the US elects a Democratic Congress, including the first socialist ever to sit in Congress, Victor L Berger of Milwaukee
William H. Frost receives the first U.S. patent for an electrical insect destroyer
An Austrian-Hungarian submarine torpedoes and sinks the Italian liner 'Ancona' without warning, killing over 200 people
Telephone Co runs 1st advertisement for Army operators, receives 7,000 applicants
Pro-German supreme commander general Cutters lay-offs
Rupert Bear by illustrator Mary Tourtel first appears in the Daily Express newspaper in the UK
Austria chancellor Ignaz Seipel, resigns after assassination attempt
Fortune Theatre opens in London
Jean Giraudoux' "Amphitryon '38" premieres in Paris
Friedrich Wolf's "Die Matrosen von Cattaro" premieres in Berlin
US PresidentFDRcreates Civil Works Administration
The Nazi exhibition Der ewige Jude ("The Eternal Jew") opens in Munich.
1st African American woman legislator, Crystal Bird Fauset of Philadelphia
Failed assassination attempt onAdolf Hitlerat Bürgerbräukeller beer hall in Munich, Germany; timebomb planted by workman Georg Elser explodes 13 minutes afterHitler's departure kills 8
RAF bombs Munich,Adolf Hitlerpromises "an attack on the capital of the Nazi movement would not go unpunished"
The Albanian Communist Party is founded
1st WW II American expeditionary force lands in Africa on the Gold Coast
Adolf Hitlerproclaims fall of Stalingrad from Munich beer hall
Vichy-France drops diplomatic relations with US
France arrests government of Lebanon after they abolish the French mandate
The Mills Brothers' single "Paper Doll" begins a 12-week run at #1; the song, written in 1915 by Johnny S. Black, becomes the vocal group's biggest hit, selling over 10 million copies
25,000 Hungarian Jews are loaned to Nazis for forced labor
Last German troops at Walcheren Island, Netherlands surrender
Riverboat sinks off Hong Kong; kills 1,550
A US aircraft shoots down a North Korean jet in the Korean War, the first jet-to-jet dogfight in history
Walt Dropo, first baseman of Boston Red Sox selected AL Rookie of Year
Salazar's party wins all parliamentary seats in Portugal
AL approves Philadelphia A's move to Kansas City
UN demands USSR leave Hungary
Great Britain performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
KJTV (now KGET) TV channel 17 in Bakersfield, CA (NBC) 1st broadcast
Tunisian president Habib Bourguiba's Nes Destour party wins every seat
Imperial Airlines Flight 201/8, a Lockheed Constellation aircraft, crashes near Richmond, Virginia, 77 die
Canada's government orders the nickel changed back to round shape
IMF grants Great Britain credit of $1 billion
KUPK TV channel 13 in Garden City, KS (ABC) begins broadcasting
British Indian Ocean Territory formed
US soap opera "Days of Our Lives" premieres
Vietnam War: US, Australian and New Zealand forces launch Operation Hump, a search-and-destroy operation near Bien Hoa in South Vietnam
Radio Leicester begins broadcasting on VHF, the 1st of 8 local British radio stations (now 40)
Silver hits a record $1.951 an ounce in London
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Londonderry Corporation agreed to a Nationalist request to introduce a points system in the allocation of public sector housing in Northern Ireland
Tom Dempsey of New Orleans Saints kicks NFL record 63 yard field goal
Premium cable channel HBO (Home Box Office) is launched in America
Nevada approves pari-mutuel betting on Jai Alai
Nick Bockwinkle beats Verne Gagne in St Paul, to become NWA champ
A series of earthquakes spreads panic in the Greek city of Thessaloniki, which is evacuated
Greek Archaeologist Manolis Andronikos rediscovers the tomb of Philip II of Macedon at Vergina
ABC broadcasts "Iran Crisis: American Held Hostage" with Frank Reynolds, a forerunner to "Nightline"
The Chilean Communist Party (Proletarian Action) is formed.
Voyager 1 space probe discovers 15th moon of Saturn
Christian Democrats looses Belgium parliamentary election
Martha Layne Collins (D) elected 1st female governor of Kentucky
STS-9 vehicle again moves to launch pad
Wilson Goode (D) elected 1st black mayor of Philadelphia
14th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Discovery (STS 51-A) launches
NASA astronaut Anna Lee Fisher becomes 1st "mom" to go into orbit aboard STS 51-A
Atlantis moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 61-B mission
11 are killed in an IRA bomb attack in Enniskillen, Northern Ireland
900 die as earthquake hits China
Arco Arena in Sacramento, California, opens, Sac Kings lose to Seattle, 97-75
Rafael Fernandez Colón re-elected Governor of Puerto Rico
"Byker Grove" begins broadcasting on the BBC
Cubs Jerome Walton wins the NL Rookie of Year
Douglas Wilder wins election for Governor of Virginia, 1st African-American elected to the office in any state
Hong Kong's MTR Lam Tin Station comes into service
100,000 additional US troops are sent to Persian gulf
Saddam fires his army chief & threatens to destroy Arabian peninsula
Unconfirmed rumors emerge that Bush might announce an airlift of supplies to US embassy in Kuwait, which could ultimately trigger a military clash
300,000 demonstrate against racism in Berlin
Cleveland Cavaliers 1st game at Gund Arena, lose to Hous Rockets, 100-98
Haitian government of Smarck Michel forms
Joe Liebermanis re-elected to the United States Senate with 67% of the vote
Republican Party nominee Tom Ridge wins Pennsylvania Gubernatorial election with 45% of the vote
Tampa Bay Devil Rays name their 1st manager Larry Rothschild
The Royal Tombs of Sipan Museum inaugurated in Lambayeque, Peru, displaying the treasure-filled tombs from the Moche culture discovered by Walter Alva
War in Iraq: More than 10,000 U.S. troops and a small number of Iraqi army units participate in a siege on the insurgent stronghold of Fallujah.

The potentially hazardous asteroid 2005 YU55 passed 0.85 lunar distances from Earth (about 324,600 kilometres or 201,700 miles), the closest known approach by an asteroid of its brightness since 2010 XC15 in 1976.
11 people are killed in a car park bombing in Mogadishu, Somalia
At least 6,000 people are killed after Typhoon Haiyan, tied with Typhoon Meranti as being the strongest storm ever recorded at landfall, hits the Philippines
Protests across New Zealand against the secretive Trans-Pacific Partnership negotiations
Brazilian surfer Rodrigo Koxa breaks the world record for surfing the largest wave ever at 24.38 meters in Nazaré, Portugal
In unprecedented move, Indian officials close all Delhi schools for rest of the week due to smog
Louvre Abu Dhabi is inaugurated, largest art museum on Arabian peninsula
UK Minister Priti Patel resigns after secret meetings revealed with Israeli officials
Authorities report more than 150 people have been killed in week-long assault by government forces on port of Hudaydah in Yemen
Azerbaijan woman who spent £16m in Harrods granted bail in UK after being arrested for suspested embezzlement under new laws
China's state news agency is the first to introduce a virtual newsreader, developed by Chinese search engine Sogou
CNN correspondent Jim Acosta's White House clearance revoked after continuing to question PresidentDonald Trumpwhile an intern tried to wrestle the microphone off him
Deadliest fire in Californian history, the Camp Fire starts at Plumas National Forest spreads in Butte County destroying town of Paradise, more than 13,000 buildings and killing at least 88
Mass grave of 200 people discovered on border of Somali and Oromia regions, Ethiopia, in investigation into atrocities by former regional president Abdi Mohammed
Qatar delivers $15 million in cash to pay civil servants in Gaza after earlier sending fuel to increase electricity from 4 to 8 hours a day
Woolsey Fire starts near Thousand Oaks, California with Malibu and Calabasas evacuated, kills three
Global recorded cases of COVID-19 pass 50 million, with the known death toll at 1,245,240 (Johns Hopkins data)
More than 50 people beheaded in latest attack by Islamist militants in Cabo Delgado province, northern Mozambique
US reopens its borders to vaccinated non US citizens after more than 18 months, lifting restrictions imposed because of COVID-19
Archaeologists announce the most significant find of Etruscan and Roman bronze statues for 50 years excavating an ancient spa in San Casciano dei Bagni, Italy
Uganda extends its three week Ebola lockdown for another two weeks, closing schools, after the death toll reaches 53, with 135 infected
European Space Telescope Euclid releases its first images - first telescope able to capture an entire galaxy in one single exposure, also built to explore dark matter and dark energy
Landmark Australian High Court ruling says a stateless Rohingya man held in detention illegally, overturning 20-year precedent allowing some asylum seekers to be held indefinitely
Darlington Public School in Sydney, Australia, is named the world's best new building at the World Architecture Festival in Singapore

The bells of Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris ring out for the first time since the 2019 fire; restored north tower belfry installation includes three new bells, one commissioned for use during the Olympic games
First meeting of Aztec EmperorMoctezuma IIand Spanish ConquistadorHernán Cortésin Tenochtitlan, Mexico
William Pennpresents the Charter of Privileges, guaranteeing religious freedom for the colony of Pennsylvania
Benjamin Franklinopens the first library in the North American colonies, the Library Company of Philadelphia
Irish revolutionaryWolfe Toneis sentenced to death by hanging for his part in the Irish Rebellion of 1798. Tone requests to be shot instead so as to die a soldier's death.
Train derails at Hightstown, New Jersey, killing two people. Also onboard areCornelius Vanderbiltand former PresidentJohn Quincy Adams.
Victor Hugo's tragic play "Ruy Blas" premieres in Paris
USS San Jacinto commanded byCharles Wilkescaptures two Confederate diplomats from the British mail steamer Trent, almost causing a war between the US and the UK
Abraham Lincoln(R) elected to his 2nd term as American President
Ciro Pinsuti's opera "Il mercante di Venezia", based onWilliam Shakespeare's "The Merchant of Venice" is first performed, at the Teatro Comunale di Bologna, Italy
French actressSarah Bernhardtmakes her US debut at NY's Booth Theater
German government recognizes KingLeopold II's Congo Free State
Grover Cleveland(D) elected 24th US President
German physicistWilhelm Röntgenproduces and detects electromagnetic radiation in a wavelength range today known as X-rays or Röntgen rays
British naval officerDavid Beattytakes part in the successful relief of the naval brigade at Tientsin during the Boxer Rebellion and is promoted to captain
Theodore Dreiser's novel "Sister Carrie" is published
American PresidentTheodore Roosevelt(R) defeats Alton B. Parker (D)
Baseball meeting to depose American League presidentBan Johnsonis set for November 12th
Adolf Hitlerand the Nazi party stage "Beer Hall Putsch" in Munich, Germany
Italian Communist leaderAntonio Gramsciis arrested at his home in Rome
George and IraGershwin's musical "Treasure Girl" premieres in NYC
Amidst the Great Depression, Democrat candidateFranklin D. Rooseveltis elected President of the United States, defeating Republican incumbentHerbert Hoover
Ford Frick, NL publicity director, is named league president
Operation Torch; began as US and British forces underEisenhowerland in French North Africa
Jean-Paul Sartre's "La Putain Respecteuse" premieres in Paris
"All The King's Men" based on Robert Penn Warren's Pulitzer winning novel, directed by Robert Rossen and starringBroderick Crawfordpremieres in New York (Best Picture 1950)
New York Yankees catcherYogi Berrawins 1st of his 3 MVP awards
Biblical drama film "The Ten Commandments", directed byCecil B. DeMille, starringCharlton HestonandYul Brynnerpremieres at the Criterion Theater in NYC

NYT criticBrooks Atkinsonwrites ofEugene O'Neill's latest play 'With the production of 'Long Day's Journey Into Night' at the Helen Hayes last evening, the American theater acquires size and stature."
John F. Kennedyis elected President of the United States, defeating Republican candidateRichard Nixonwho was the incumbent Vice President
Whitey Fordis voted Cy Young Award winner overWarren Spahn
Edward W. Brooke(Rep-R-Mass) becomes 1st African American popularly elected to the US Senate
Frank Robinson, Baltimore Orioles outfielder, selected as AL MVP,first player to win MVP in both leagues
Movie actorRonald Reaganis elected Governor of California
PresidentLyndon B. Johnsonsigns anti-trust immunity to AFL-NFL merger
Stock Exchange Tower, the new 26-story home of the London Stock Exchange opened byQueen Elizabeth II
"Greatest Hits" 11th studio album byElton Johnis released
Ted Bundyvictim Debi Kent disappears in Salt Lake City, Utah
Ed Kochis elected Mayor of New York City (his 1st term)
Tom Stoppard's play "Night & Day" premieres in London
ICC Men's Cricket World Cup, Eden Gardens, Kolkata, India: Player of the matchDavid Boonscores 75 as Australia beats England by 7 runs for their first title
Joe Liebermanis elected to the United States Senate as a Democrat, defeating incumbent Lowell Weicker
Vice President George H. W. Bush is elected President of the United States, defeating Democrat candidateMichael Dukakis
Darryl Strawberrysigns 5-year contract with LA Dodgers
MusicianChris Isaakreleases single "Wicked Game"
Paul Coffeysets NHL defenseman scoring mark with 311th goal

Nigel Mansellin a Williams crashes during the season ending Australian Grand Prix in Adelaide but wins his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 52 points; Mansell won record 9 GPs during the season

Arista Records releasesDavid Bowie's 19th studio "The Buddha of Suburbia", based on the novel of the same name by Hanif Kureishi, in the UK and Europe only; it is not released in US until nearly 2 years later
Voters in California elect entertainerSonny Bonoto US Congress
"The World is Not Enough" 19th James Bond film premieres in Los Angeles, starringPierce Brosnan,Robert Carlyle,Robbie Coltrane, and Denise Richards
TenorAndrea Bocellireleases his "Sacred Arias" album, the world's best-selling classical album by a solo artist
UN Security Council unanimously approves Resolution 1441 on Iraq, forcingSaddam Husseinto disarm or face "serious consequences"
Mercury Nashville Records releases "Greatest Hits" album byShania Twain(Billboard Album of the Year, 2005)
American/Columbia records releases "12 Songs", the twenty-sixth studio album byNeil Diamond; the album produced by Rick Rubin is one of the most critically acclaimed of his career
EllenJohnson-Sirleafis elected president of Liberia, the first woman to lead an African country
John Keyleads the National Party to victory in New Zealand's general election, defeating Prime MinisterHelen Clarkand her Labour-led coalition after nine years government
Mikhail Gorbachevwarns that tensions between America and Russia over Ukraine have put the world on the brink of a new Cold War
US PresidentBarack ObamanominatesLoretta Lynchfor the position of US Attorney General
USPresident Obamaauthorises deployment of 1,500 additional troops to help train and advise Iraqi and Kurdish forces fighting Islamic State militants

Indian PMNarendra Modiannounces the demonetization of all ₹500 and ₹1,000 banknotes during an unscheduled live televised address, canceling 86% of the notes in circulation in a battle against corruption

RepublicanDonald Trumpis elected 45th President of the United States, defeating DemocratHillary Clintonwith an Electoral College victory of 304-227; Clinton receives just under 2.9 million more popular votes
First test of a high-speed levitating pod system to carry people and cargo, byRichard Branson's Virgin Hyperloop in Las Vegas, Nevada
ActorChris Evansnamed People magazine’s Sexiest Man Alive, says “My mom will be so happy”
Kathy Hochulbecomes the first elected female Governor of New York, continuing in the role she held since replacing Andrew Cuomo mid-term
George Gershwin, Ira Gershwin, andP. G. Wodehouse's musical "Oh, Kay!" opens at the Imperial Theatre, NYC; runs for 256 performances
"Mutiny on the Bounty" directed by Frank Lloyd and starring Charles Laughton andClark Gablepremieres in New York (Best Production/Picture 1936)
British peer the Earl of Lucan disappears and is never seen again after his nanny is found murdered in London
